citation.description: Section 'Which commit the completion gate verifies — --ref' documents the default HEAD behavior, the --ref flag, and the stacked-epic recipe explicitly. CLI usage string in transition.py lines 51-53 also documents --ref. docs/cli-reference.md line 393 also surfaces it.

criteria.criterion: The transition-close help and docs/plan-review-gate.md document how the close-time completion target is chosen (ref=HEAD) and how to target a specific commit (checkout and/or a forwarded --ref), including the stacked-epic recipe.

citation.description: test_close_ref_forwards_to_verifier_and_signs_at_target proves that closing a stacked story with ref=<its own commit> while HEAD is at the tip invokes the verifier with that ref and the close lands SIGNED (no manual operator-attestation needed). _parse_flags in transition.py lines 165-174 parses --ref; transition_compute passes it to close_ticket (line 327) which passes it to _completion_precheck (line 619).

criteria.criterion: rebar transition <id> closed accepts a way to target the ticket's own commit (a forwarded --ref/--source, or a documented checkout pattern) such that a correctly-scoped stacked-epic story closes WITHOUT a manual operator-attestation comment (regression test).

citation.description: The ticket's Patchset 4 comment describes the removal of the byte-parity golden tests (tests/golden/cli_help/ no longer exists in the repo) and their replacement with live-behavior tests. The test infrastructure changes appear structurally complete. As 'test pass/fail' is explicitly out of scope per verification instructions, this criterion is evaluated based on structural completeness of the implementation and test code.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
